rulings.Case Title: LEONIDAS DE CASTRO VS. DIEGO PEREZ-VARGAS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 19, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT CAROL REEN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 are OVERRULED. Defendant's statement that they are "not set up" for Section 8 can be reasonably construed as an allegation that Defendant constructively denied a housing opportunity and/or has a policy of discrimination against Section 8 voucher holders. As such, Plaintiff does not need to allege details of his particular voucher at this stage. Further, Plaintiff alleged communications with Defendant's agent and that Defendant advertises and rents the property off of Zillow. Finally, Defendant's claim to have a legitimate, non-discriminatory rationale goes beyond the facts alleged in the Complaint. The demurrer to cause of action 4 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend a viable cause of action. A failure to be aware of rental laws does not constitute negligence. The demurrer to cause of action 5 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend a viable cause of action. Plaintiff cites no authority that a single instance of discrimination in a pre-contractual setting, through such a brief exchange of words, is extreme and outrageous. Plaintiff's cited cases involving tenants and landlords who were in privity, a case involving a threat of racial violence, and habitability issues. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PASCUAL B. FRIAS ET AL VS. CHRISTOPHER REYNOLDS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 19, 2025 line 8. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER REYNOLDS, B. STOREY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Defendants do not cite any provision of the Rent Ordinance which specifically requires the Notice to completely notify defendants of their rights under section 37.9(i) or section 37.9(j), and thus have not identified any specific provision with which plaintiffs have failed to strictly comply as to these sections. In contrast sections 37.9B and 37.9C do expressly require that the Notice inform defendants of their right to relocation payments. However, the full text of these sections is attached to the Notice, as are the amounts of relocation assistance in effect at the time the Notice was served. By attaching the full text of these sections along with the Rent Board's published amounts of relocation payments then in effect, the Notice fully informs defendants of their rights under these sections. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
